error_message: Drop cap overprints body text on ~14 chapter openers (worst: ch_17, coda)
root_cause: LaTeX lettrine shapes only ONE paragraph; when the opening paragraph ran shorter than the 3-line drop cap, the next paragraph returned to full measure and printed through the glyph. Separate sub-bug: single-letter first words ('A year on...') made rest.find(' ')==0, dumping the whole paragraph into lettrine's small-caps argument.
fix: build_kdp.py drop_cap(): join short openers (<210 visible chars) to following paragraph(s) inside one TeX paragraph via \hfill\break\hspace*{1.4em}; handle rest[:1]==' ' single-letter case with empty word_rest.

error_message: Reversed opening quotes in print wherever dialogue opens with italic text (”Human, ”Firing, ...)
root_cause: smart() ran per-segment AFTER the italic split, so a quote at segment end (before *italics*) failed the (?=\w) lookahead and fell through to the closing-quote default.
fix: New dquotes() resolves straight double quotes with full-line context BEFORE the italic split (open after start/whitespace/dash/paren, else close).

error_message: Mid-chapter markdown blockquotes typeset with literal '> ' glyphs (40 lines: ch_18 debate, ch_27 letter)
root_cause: body_to_tex() had no blockquote handling; only the opener transmission was consumed by split_chapter.
fix: body_to_tex() groups '^>' runs into a new machineblock list environment (indented, \small, parskip).

error_message: Cover PDF became 2 pages with page 1 blank after extending spine rules to full bleed height
root_cause: TikZ stroke extends half a line width beyond the path; rules drawn 0..H grew the picture bounding box 0.4pt past the page, pushing the inline picture to page 2.
fix: Mark the full-height rules [overlay] so they don't enter the bounding box.

error_message: fontspec: 'WordSpace font feature cannot be used in \addfontfeatures'
fix: Define a dedicated \titlefont family with WordSpace=1.45 instead of \addfontfeature at use site.

error_message: Persistent 11.9pt overfull after closing up em dashes (XeTeX wouldn't break after the dash)
root_cause: Closed-up em dashes removed break-friendly spaces; combined with tight measure the para had no feasible break. \XeTeXdashbreakstate alone did not resolve it.
